data.title: Poly Group: We have not organized or participated in any business or activities related to Hong Kong stablecoins or stablecoin funds.
data.description: According to Sina Finance, China Poly Group has issued a statement regarding recent online rumors regarding the "Poly Stablecoin in Hong Kong," the "Poly Stablecoin Fund," and related entities. The statement stated that Poly Group and its subsidiaries have not organized or participated in any business or activities related to the Hong Kong Stablecoin or Stablecoin Fund. Hong Kong-registered companies "Poly Digital Industry Group Co., Ltd.", "Poly Digital Asset Co., Ltd.", and "Poly Digital Asset Issuance Co., Ltd." have no equity or affiliation with Poly Group or its subsidiaries, nor do they have any investment, cooperation, or business relationships. All actions taken by these companies are unrelated to Poly Group. Poly Group urges all sectors of society to remain vigilant, exercise due diligence, and exercise caution in investment and cooperation. If any illegal or criminal activity is discovered, please report it to the public security authorities immediately.

data.title: Planet Morning News
data.description: 1. Inflation data reinforced expectations of a rate cut, with traders betting on two more Fed rate cuts this year; 2. Funders for the $300 million renovation of the Trump White House ballroom include crypto companies such as Ripple and Tether; 3. Crypto mining company TeraWulf completed a $3.2 billion bond issuance, intended to be used for data center expansion; 4. The "mysterious whale with a 100% winning rate" has increased its long position value to $270 million, with a floating loss of $384,000; 5. Swiss pure Bitcoin investment app Relai obtained an EU license under MiCA; 6. The White House stated that inflation data may not be released next month, which would be a first in history; 7. Crypto.com submitted an application for a national trust bank charter to the U.S. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency.

data.title: Bank of England probes data-mining lending strategies fueling AI bets
data.description:
The Bank of England is worried that a rise in financiers' lending to data center lending may cause an AI bubble reminiscent of the dot-com crash in the early 2000s.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bank-of-england-probes-data-mining-lending?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Core Scientific Reports Decline in Bitcoin Mining Output for Q3 2025
data.description:
According to PANews, Core Scientific, a Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company, has released its financial results for the third quarter of 2025. The report reveals a 55% decrease in Bitcoin mining output compared to the previous year. The company's self-mined digital asset revenue amounted to $57.4 million, down from $68.1 million in the same period last year. Additionally, revenue from hosted mining services was $8.7 million, also lower than the previous year's figures.

Core Scientific also disclosed that by the end of the third quarter, it held $453.4 million in cash and cash equivalents, along with Bitcoin valued at $241.4 million.

data.title: Bank of England Investigates AI Lending Amid Financial Stability Concerns
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, the Bank of England (BOE) is conducting an investigation into the increasing trend of financiers lending to data centers, a move seen as speculative on the future of artificial intelligence (AI). This inquiry comes amid concerns that AI companies may not meet their high valuations, potentially leading to a market correction similar to the dot-com bubble of the early 2000s.

The BOE is examining the relationship between AI firms and financiers eager to invest in the AI sector. Although lending to data centers remains a niche market, it is expected to become a vital funding source, with McKinsey & Co estimating that $6.7 trillion will be required by 2030 to meet the growing demand for AI infrastructure. This investigation was initiated after the BOE observed a shift in funds from hiring staff to investing billions in data center construction.

With limited AI-native stocks and the crypto tokenization of private AI stocks not yet scalable, data-center lending has emerged as one of the few avenues for substantial investment in the AI industry. However, the BOE's probe could lead to future regulatory constraints, potentially affecting returns and slowing AI innovation. UK crypto groups have criticized the BOE's proposal to limit individual stablecoin holdings, arguing that it is restrictive and costly to implement. Despite assurances that these restrictions are not permanent, UK banks have imposed their own measures, with 40% of surveyed crypto investors reporting payment delays or blocks by their banks.

The BOE is concerned that these emerging lending practices could pose risks to financial stability. It stated that if the anticipated scale of debt-financed AI and related energy infrastructure investment materializes over the next decade, financial stability risks are likely to increase. Banks could be directly exposed through their credit exposures to AI companies and indirectly through loans and credit facilities to private credit funds and other financial institutions affected by AI-related asset prices.

data.title: Rumble integrates Bitcoin tipping with Tether
data.description:
Rumble, a video platform similar to YouTube, is collaborating with Tether to enable creators to receive Bitcoin tips. The company announced the move at the Plan B Forum in Lugano, stating that it aims to help creators earn and remain independent with crypto. Tether, which has already committed a sizeable investment in Rumble, is seen […]

data.title: The "40x Short BTC" address increased its short position to 550 BTC, equivalent to approximately $60.95 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi (@ai_9684xtpa), the "100% win rate whale" counterparty "40x short BTC" address remained firm in its position after experiencing the sharp fluctuations in CPI last night. It has now increased its short position to 550 BTC (approximately US$60.95 million), with an opening price of US$109,621 and a current floating loss of US$667,000.

data.title: Interpol and Afripol Arrest 83 in Anti-Terrorism Financing Operation
data.description: According to PANews, a joint operation by Interpol and Afripol targeting terrorism financing and illegal activities has led to the arrest of 83 individuals across six African countries. Among those detained, 19 were apprehended for the 'illegal use' of virtual assets. During the two-month operation, authorities from participating countries screened over 15,000 individuals and entities, uncovering approximately $260 million in fiat and virtual currencies potentially linked to terrorist activities. Around $600,000 has been seized so far, with ongoing investigations aimed at tracking and recovering additional assets.

data.title: Zhang Yiming suddenly became a Singaporean citizen, and ByteDance became a "Singaporean company"
data.description:
On the evening of April 1st, Singapore time, Lianhe Zaobao confirmed that ByteDance co-founder Zhang Yiming has officially become a Singaporean citizen. ByteDance also stated that the company has become a "Singaporean company whose actual controller is a Singaporean citizen."

With this change of citizenship, Zhang Yiming climbed to 23rd place on the 2025 Forbes Global Billionaires List with a net worth of US$65.5 billion, becoming Singapore's new richest person and the only Singaporean to make the top 50. His wealth increased by nearly 51% year-on-year. As the driving force behind TikTok, he helped the platform reach over 1.2 billion users worldwide and stepped down as ByteDance's president and chairman in 2021.

This sudden naturalization not only made Zhang Yiming the wealthiest entrepreneur in Singapore but also thrust ByteDance's corporate identity back into the international spotlight.

data.title: Tether CEO: Expected profit of $15 billion this year, with a profit margin of up to 99%
data.description: Tether CEO Paolo Ardoino recently stated in an interview at a forum in Switzerland that Tether's profits this year will be close to $15 billion, with a profit margin of 99%. When asked about the types of investors he'd like to attract, he said that many companies in the portfolios of funds and tech funds could potentially use some of Tether's technology and products, emphasizing synergies and greater impact. However, he did not disclose the names of potential investors.

data.description:
China Poly Group issues a statement denying any involvement in alleged Hong Kong stablecoin activities as rumors sp
source: https://coincu.com/news/poly-group-denies-hk-stablecoin/

data.title: Weekly deal volume x402 increased 492.63% month-on-month.
data.description: According to data from Dune, the number of weekly transactions on the x402 protocol increased by 492.63% compared to the previous week, reaching a record high of 156,492 transactions. x402 is an open-source payment protocol launched by Coinbase in early 2025. The protocol implements a blockchain-agnostic micropayment system based on the HTTP 402 status code. Designed for AI agents and automated payments, the protocol supports instant payments using stablecoins like USDC, eliminating the need for account registration, membership registration, or complex signature processes.

data.title: Tether plans to launch compliant stablecoin USAT in December
data.description: Tether plans to launch its USAT stablecoin in December for the US market, complying with federal regulations under the GENIUS Act. The token will be issued by Tether America, a joint venture between Tether and Anchorage Digital, a regulated US crypto bank. Tether CEO Paolo Ardoino stated that the company is investing in expanding USAT's user base, aiming to reach 100 million US users.

data.title: Citigroup lowered its target price for Gemini to $23 and raised its price target for Bullish to $77.
data.description: According to Coindesk, Citigroup lowered its price target for Gemini (GEMI), a cryptocurrency exchange with a neutral rating, from $26 to $23. Meanwhile, it raised its forecast for Bullish, which it had a buy rating, from $70 to $77. The bank's analysts stated that despite strong credit card registrations and app downloads, trading volume in October fell short of expectations, suggesting that growth for the exchange will take longer. Bullish's approval of a New York BitLicense and expanded institutional access indicate strong momentum.

data.title: A whale that has been silent for three months received 1,030 BTC from FalconX, worth approximately $114 million.
data.description: According to OnchainLens monitoring, a whale received 1,030 BTC from FalconX after being silent for 3 months, worth approximately $114 million.

data.title: U.S. Banks Expand Tokenized Deposit Network Nationwide
data.description: According to PANews, Custodia Bank and Vantage Bank Texas have launched a tokenized deposit real-time network for U.S. banks, expanding their previous pilot to a nationwide network. This system facilitates switching between deposits and stablecoins compliant with the GENIUS Act. The platform is currently awaiting final regulatory approval for large-scale implementation.

data.title: Crypto miners rally as Jane Street discloses major positions
data.description:
Jane Street reports new 13G filings showing large investments in Bitcoin mining firms.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/jane-street-discloses-major-positions/

data.title: Crypto Miners See Stock Surge Following Jane Street's Investment
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, shares of several cryptocurrency mining companies experienced significant gains following the disclosure of substantial investments by trading firm Jane Street. The firm revealed its passive trading positions in Bitfarms, Cipher Mining, and Hut 8, owning approximately 5.4%, 5%, and 5% of each company, respectively. This announcement led to a notable rally in the stocks of these companies, with increases ranging from 8% to 13% on Thursday, continuing into Friday.

By the close of the market on Friday, Bitfarms (BITF) saw a rise of 10.68%, Cipher Mining (CIFR) surged by 19.73%, and Hut 8 (HUT) increased by approximately 17.27%, as reported by Yahoo Finance. Other Bitcoin mining stocks also benefited from the positive sentiment, with American Bitcoin Corp. climbing 11.29%, IREN Limited up 12.60%, and Hive Digital Technologies advancing 17.77%. Jane Street, known for its proprietary trading and market-making activities in equities and digital assets, initially disclosed its involvement with Bitcoin miners in 2023 through investments in Marathon Digital (MARA).

The recent disclosure by Jane Street follows Google's acquisition of a 5.4% stake in Cipher Mining on September 25. The Bitcoin mining sector, crucial for maintaining the network's functionality and security, involves using specialized computers to solve complex mathematical problems. This process verifies transactions and introduces new Bitcoin into circulation. While individual miners occasionally succeed in mining a block, the industry is increasingly dominated by larger mining companies, many of which have experienced substantial growth in 2025.

Over the past year, Bitcoin mining companies have outperformed Bitcoin itself. Data from Yahoo Finance indicates that Bitfarms has surged nearly 131%, and Hut 8 has risen approximately 211% over the past 12 months. In comparison, Bitcoin has increased by about 73% over the same period. This trend highlights the growing influence and success of Bitcoin mining companies in the current market landscape.
